posts.author: mrdjallen (DJ Allen)
posts.cookedHtml: We would love to ability to have a simple “if text contains” formula that could update a status column. For instance: if a text contains “ABC” then update status to “ABC”. This would help us use the color-coding that the status column offers to keep better track of our tasks. I realize this is a pretty simple feature request, but it would help us in many of our processes, especially as our boards grow and we rely more heavily on at-a-glance views of our boards.

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
I need to set up a vehicle maintenance fleet using kilometers as input values.

COLUMN NUMBER A = KILOMETERS OF REAL VEHICLE
COLUMN NUMBER B = OBJECTIVE VEHICLE KILOMETERS FOR A CERTAIN SERVICE
COLUMN OF FORMULA = B - A (HOW MANY KILOMETERS TO ARRIVE FOR THIS SERVICE)

Things I can’t do with Monday.com (missing recipes)

WHEN A FORMULA / NUMBER (could be either of these two) IS GREATER / EQUAL / LESS THAN THIS FORMULA / NUMBER (could be any) THEN:

  • NOTIFY SOMEONE
  • CHANGE OF STATUS TO XXX

Automations (normal and custom recipes) must support ASAP columns of numbers and formulas to automate actions based on logical operations.

Think for a minute about the only avaliable recipe that supports number columns:

  • A column of numbers will always be a fixed number (at least from Monday.com it cannot be changed with any formula or automation). So, I have a fixed value, and I can only compare it with another fixed value!!! … from all points of view, I can’t find a use for this.

Following this thread and looking to do something similar… based on a Status field, assign a formula field a number (score). Having some issues with the formula though… any insight into what I’m doing wrong?

posts.author: babacita (John Kowalski)
posts.cookedHtml:
Disregard my above… Monday.com helped out. Here’s the correct formula:

IF({Sales Stage Formula}=“1 - Lead”, “3”, IF({Sales Stage Formula}=“6 - Close”, “10”, IF({Sales Stage Formula}=“2 - Qualification”, “5”, IF({Sales Stage Formula}=“3 - Application/Test/Evaluation”,“7”))))

Happy day!

Screenshot 2021-05-05 at 14.23.331340×132 15.1 KB

posts.author: andrewalmand (Andrew Almand)
posts.cookedHtml:
@jonathandbem

You will need to make sure your column names within the “{}” is accurate but this formula should work:

ROUND(({item’s Est. Time}-(VALUE(HOUR({item’s Time Tracking})+(MINUTE({item’s Time Tracking})/60)))),3)

You have to use HOUR & MINUTE to convert the 01:05 into a number. Dividing the 05 by 60 in order to turn it into minutes then adding that decimal to the hour getting 1.083. Then use VALUE on the whole thing so it a number and you can subtract it from your Est. Time. Then finally rounding the answer to your desired number of decimal places(I did 3 but you can adjust that as needed).

Earlier I mentioned making the output column a Text but you will want to change it back to Numbers so that this will work in your automations. The result populates properly in both column types but only Numbers is supported by the automations you need.

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Its important to understand this is an architectural hurdle for monday. Formulas are calculated on the fly on the client side, and the results of formulas are not stored. This is the reason they are no included in automations.

You can also utilize some free-plan apps such as General Caster or Column Magic to cast formulas to standard monday columns that can be used in automations.

Thanks
Mark

posts.author: JackNagy (Jack Nagy)
posts.cookedHtml:
The issue is that the Formula column is one of the major selling points of the Pro & above plans, and virtually everybody (as we can tell from this thread!) will naturally assume that automations can be applied to those columns — this is how the rest of Monday.com behaves, and this is how competing services that offer Formula columns behave.

Additionally, this is arguably the largest usecase for formula columns; I would wager that the number of people who bought the Pro plan for formulas just to sum, divide, etc. is relatively small (because these functions are often accomplishable with the column summaries & dashboards) compared to the number of people who bought the formulas capability expecting to be able to automate with it.

Accordingly, this is accordingly a pretty jarring hurdle to encounter.

I think everyone here understands that it would be difficult for Monday.com to make work given a) that formulas are currently driven client-side, and b) the Apps ecosystem has developers like General Caster who are reliant on this functionality being broken in the native app.

But, I mean… the flip side is that everyone upvoting this thread is feeling like they have effectively been told “hey, we sold you a plan on this functionality… but to actually use it in a standard or expected way for a PM tool, you’re going to need to pay an external developer extra, double your amount of columns & automations to build and maintain (1 for formula, 1 for General Caster), and write your formulas in a different syntax.”

Don’t get me wrong, I love Monday.com. They do a great job. Lots of cool functionality that other tools don’t have. But that just makes it all the weirder that they’ve chosen to implement this functionality, a key selling point, in a non-standard way that introduces so much hassle for users.

It’d be great to have an option to perform formulas on the Monday.com servers (maybe these can be limited per month, by plan?) so we can then actually make use of the Formula column as we wanted to.

Sadly for everyone here wondering how to use Formulas with Automations, it is sadly not possible for the moment. The Formula is only calculated at the rendering (what you see) of the page, which is more commonly called the Front-End. Unlike other columns which are Back-End (What happens behind the scene) based.

It will require a lot of work from the team over at Monday to make these work with automations.

posts.author: CarbonJon (Jon)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Wonderful Monday Community!

First and foremost, we’ve been keenly following the discussions here and have noted the overwhelming requests for a specific feature that monday.com doesn’t natively offer. We listened, we acted, and today, we’re beyond excited to share some news with you all!

Announcing for the First Time - The CarbonToolbelt!

One of the standout features of CarbonToolbelt is its ability to use formulas and automatically cast them to columns within an automation. Imagine the convenience of using complex formulas right within your automations, and the results automatically appearing in the desired columns, without any additional steps. We’ve taken the magic of monday.com and given it an extra sprinkle of efficiency!

Deep Dive into the Feature:

  • Seamless Integration: CarbonToolbelt integrates effortlessly with your existing Monday boards.
  • Advanced Formulas: Go beyond the basic calculations. Whether you need mathematical operations, text manipulations, or even date calculations, we’ve got you covered.
  • Auto-casting: No more manual transfers. Once your formula is set within an automation, the result will be automatically cast to your desired column.

Ready to Try?

Dive right into the CarbonToolbelt experience with our Download Link. Get a hands-on experience of the seamless formula integration and auto-casting functionality.

Want a Closer Look?

We understand that a new tool can bring up questions, and we’re here to walk you through it! Book a Demo Meeting with our experts, and let us guide you through the numerous capabilities of CarbonToolbelt.

Why CarbonToolbelt?

While this announcement shines a spotlight on the much-requested formula feature, CarbonToolbelt is packed with many other integrations that amplify the power of monday.com. It’s designed to fill gaps, enhance your workflow, and make your Monday experience smoother than ever!

To everyone who voiced their need for this feature: Thank you! Your feedback drives innovation, and CarbonToolbelt is a testament to this wonderful community’s collaborative spirit.

Here’s to making monday.com even more powerful! Can’t wait for you all to try CarbonToolbelt and share your feedback.

Cheers and happy automating!

Warm Regards, The CarbonWeb Team

posts.author: knesbitt (Kyle Nesbitt)
posts.cookedHtml:
We need automations to recognize formulas!
I’m a process developer for my company and I have spent the last 11 months trying to become the resident “expert” in Monday.com here. With that being said, any time I introduce a software to my processes my goal is to make the software work for us, not the other way around.

We are a manufacturing/construction company and our world revolves around the “Need on site” date. We then use formulas to work backwards to give us our material order by date, when to start fabrication, etc.

I have rebuilt our current excel tool into Monday.com using the formula columns and it works great. The only down side is that even though I format my formulas as dates, Monday doesn’t recognize them as dates and automations don’t work. Yes, I could input date columns into the board but then my co-workers will have to enter all of the dates manually which is inefficient and dates aren’t dynamic. With around 15 projects going on, each having a board like the one I mentioned, there is a high risk of dates being missed without an alert system (automations).

There are many other application for this to be helpful beyond dates. I hope there are other power users who have the same pain that can help make this a high priority.

posts.author: Carson (Carson Butcher)
posts.cookedHtml:
For those exploring this thread hoping to find a way to implement formulas in automation and are tired of waiting, look no further. (No, this isn’t native monday, but it’s as close as can be!)

CarbonToolbelt provides this exact functionality, albeit slightly different than monday’s native formula column.

You’re able to trigger formulas based on actions, perform the calculation, and then store the result in your desired column. This means you’re now able to mirror this data and use it deeper in your workflow.

Aside from formula capabilities, the toolbelt has 20+ other automation recipes that’ll unlock thousands of possibilities within your monday workflows.

Some notable capabilities:

Date Changes - Push dates forward while keeping set times intact

Formulas & Calculations - Break free from formula column limitations. Store calculated values anywhere.

Item Updates - Copy-paste updates, move items, and create custom checklists

Required Columns - Check for required column values and establish clear SOPs

Manipulate Columns - Automatically copy, change, and update columns and mirrored columns.

Assign People - Assign a person or team intuitively with automation triggers

posts.author: paulmatsushima (Paul Matsushima)
posts.cookedHtml:
@hobbit - I definitely agree with your frustration that our companies pay so much for Monday.com yet many basic features/fixes go unaddressed by their dev team. I’m guessing that their dev team, like many Saas companies out there, prioritize new and exciting features like the Work Canvas or AI over fixes to basic functionality like formulas in automations. The new features are what attract new customers.

There are so many fixes to basic features I want the dev team to address that many people have been asking for. But they go unaddressed because of the lack of votes or because a third-party solution has been built in the Monday marketplace. That’s additionally frustrating because you have to pay more.

Here’s just a quick list of fixes I hope they address:

  1. Item Description - want the ability to take live notes with full markdown formatting (without using a work doc) on an item. But “Item description” is only available on the Monday dev product or as a paid third-party app (request here)
  2. Long text field - 2000 character limit and no rich text abilities (request here)
  3. Item card doesn’t come pre-installed in the Table view (request here)
  4. Items - can’t be shared across multiple boards (request here)
  5. Email automations - can’t add attachments and no ability for rich text formatting
  6. Connect an item to another board automations can’t be upserted (ie. update a record if it exists and create one if it doesn’t), which creates many duplicates in your Sales CRM contacts and accounts boards. (request here)
  7. The “dedupe” feature doesn’t really merge two items together, it just archives one and doesn’t copy the said item’s column info into the non-archived item.
  8. Move updates to a different item (request here)

posts.author: Nick_G_TSE (Nick Granzow)
posts.cookedHtml:
The issue here is company growth. Big, exciting features are prioritized because they attract new customers to the platform, which is especially important for a publicly traded company like monday.com. The marketplace is also a revenue stream, so if they can avoid creating a native feature by offering a marketplace option instead, they can generate more revenue. This approach often leads to pushing users to buy expensive apps rather than developing the feature natively.

They hope the added cost isn’t enough to make you leave monday.com, factoring in some expected customer loss, but not enough to prioritize these features. However, I disagree with this approach. While shareholders benefit from increased revenue, I believe customers should come first. The marketplace should cater to very complex needs, not basic functionalities like this one.

One example of a feature that should be natively supported is HTML automated emails. It’s 2024, and we are still stuck with plain text emails. This feature is available in the marketplace for a hefty fee, despite being a basic need that has been discussed for a long time and is still not even on the roadmap.

posts.author: KlarcAP (Klarc)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello, Monday community!

I would like to share an idea that I believe could significantly improve how we use Monday in our daily tasks. Currently, the lack of flexibility in formula columns greatly limits the ability to create new connections and automations that could greatly enhance our work efficiency.

Current Problem

At present, values from formula columns cannot be directly used in Monday’s native automations. This forces us to seek alternative solutions that are often complicated, require additional manual work, or depend on external integrations.

Proposed Solution

My suggestion is to allow the values generated by formula columns to be used directly in Monday’s native automations. With this functionality, we could:

  • Create more sophisticated and customized automations.
  • Connect data between boards more efficiently.
  • Reduce the need for auxiliary columns and manual processes.
  • Increase productivity and data accuracy.

Expected Benefits

This improvement would bring several benefits to all users, such as:

  • Greater flexibility in creating automated workflows.
  • Ability to make decisions based on real-time calculated data.
  • Better integration between different areas and processes within the platform.

I believe this functionality would be a great advancement for all of us, providing an even more efficient and connected work environment.

Thank you for your attention, and I hope to see this improvement implemented soon. Let’s continue collaborating to make Monday better and better!

Thank you!

posts.author: Tony_Luna (Antonio Luna Mariño)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, I had a success story with an automation recipe using a formula column as a baseline.

In advance, an apology if the English is not a better translation.

I also share the case in English below.

Antonio Luna
Project Manager
Guadalajara, Jalisco. México
February 13, 2025

At the Autonomous University of Guadalajara

Success Story: Automation Recipe Using Fx (ENGLISH)

Context

My goal was to streamline the process of receiving and managing customer requests for editorial material production services. To achieve this, I implemented a solution that automatically evaluates the feasibility of each request and routes it to the appropriate team.

Detailed Process

  1. Request Reception: Customers fill out an online form, specifying their desired delivery date for the material. This date is captured in a board column designated as “DATE” (column type: DATE).
  2. Feasibility Calculation:
  • Formula Column 1 (Production Duration): I calculated the estimated production duration in business days for the Editorial Service (a predefined value) of 12 business days.
  • Formula Column 2 (Estimated Delivery Date): I determined the estimated delivery date by adding the production duration (Formula 1) to the request creation date: (WORKDAY({Creation}, {formula 1})).
  • Approval Column: I compared the customer’s desired delivery “DATE” with the “Estimated Delivery Date” (Formula 2). If the desired date is earlier than the estimated date, the request is marked as “NOT FEASIBLE”; otherwise, it is marked as “FEASIBLE” (IF(WORKDAYS({DATE}, {formula 2}) > 0, “FEASIBLE”, “NOT FEASIBLE”)).
  1. Automation Recipe Creation Using Fx:
  • Problem: Initially, I was unable to create automation recipes based on the “APPROVAL” formula column.
  • Solution:
    • Update-Generating Automation: I created an automation that generates an update on the item, assigning the “APPROVAL” column in the message body. This will create an update saying “FEASIBLE” or “NOT FEASIBLE”.
    • Automation Recipe: When an item is created, Then create an update with this text (“FEASIBLE” or “NOT FEASIBLE”, depending on what the APPROVAL column indicates).
    • Text Column with AI Autocomplete: I created a text column and used the “AI Autocomplete” function to extract the text from the item update, which contains the feasibility status.
    • Status Column with AI Autocomplete: I created a status column and configured it to be automatically completed with AI, classifying the text from the previous column into “FEASIBLE” (green) or “NOT FEASIBLE” (red).
    • Final Automation: I created an automation recipe based in a formula column that: when the status of an item changes to “FEASIBLE”, automatically moves it to group 2 (the service team’s space).

Results

I successfully automated the feasibility evaluation of customer requests and the transfer of feasible requests to the service team, which streamlines the process and reduces the need for manual intervention.

Lessons Learned and Improvements

  • Use of AI: The combination of automatic updates and text/status columns with AI autocomplete proved to be an effective solution to overcome the limitations of automations regarding their implementation in formulas.

ESPANISH

Caso de Éxito: Receta de automatización utilizando Fx (ESPAÑOL)

Contexto

Mi objetivo era agilizar el proceso de recepción y gestión de solicitudes de clientes que requieren un servicio de producción de material editorial. Para ello, implementé una solución que evalúa automáticamente la viabilidad de cada solicitud y la deriva al equipo correspondiente.

Proceso Detallado

  1. Recepción de Solicitudes: Los clientes completan el formulario en línea, donde especifican la fecha de entrega deseada para el material. Esta fecha se captura en una columna del tablero designada como “FECHA” (tipo de columna: FECHA).
  2. Cálculo de Viabilidad:
  • Columna de Fórmula 1 (Duración de Producción): Calculé la duración estimada de producción en días hábiles del Servicio Editorial (un valor predefinido) por 12 días hábiles.
  • Columna de Fórmula 2 (Fecha de Entrega Estimada): Determiné la fecha de entrega estimada sumando la duración de producción (Fórmula 1) a la fecha de creación de la solicitud: (WORKDAY({Creación}, {fórmula 1})).
  • Columna de Aprobación: Comparé la “FECHA” de entrega deseada por el cliente con la “Fecha de Entrega Estimada” (Fórmula 2). Si la fecha deseada es menor a la fecha estimada, la solicitud se marca como “NO VIABLE”; de lo contrario, se marca como “VIABLE” (IF(WORKDAYS({FECHA}, {fórmula 2}) > 0, “VIABLE”, “NO VIABLE”)).
  1. Creación de receta de Automatización usando Fx:*
  • Problema: Inicialmente, no podía crear recetas de automatización basadas en la columna de fórmula “APROBACIÓN”.
  • Solución:*
    • Automatización generadora de update: Cree una automatización que genera una update en el elemento, asigné la columna “APROBACIÓN” en el cuerpo del mensaje, por lo que creará una update que diga “VIABLE” o “NO VIABLE”.
      Recete de automatización: When an item is created, Then create an update with this text (VIABLE" o "NO VIABLE, según lo que indique la columna de APROBACIÓ).
    • Columna tipo Texto con autocompletar de la IA: Cree una columna de texto y utilicé la función “Autocompletar con IA” para extraer el texto de la actualización del elemento, que contiene el estado de viabilidad.
    • Columna tipo Estado con autocompletar de la IA: Cree una columna de estado y la configuré para que se complete automáticamente con IA, clasificando el texto de la columna anterior en “VIABLE” (verde) o “NO VIABLE” (rojo).
    • Automatización Final: Cree una receta de automatización que, cuando el estado de un elemento cambia a “VIABLE”, lo mueve automáticamente al grupo 2 (el espacio del equipo de servicio).

Resultados

Logré automatizar la evaluación de viabilidad de las solicitudes de clientes y la transferencia de las solicitudes viables al equipo de servicio, lo que agiliza el proceso y reduce la necesidad de intervención manual.

Lecciones Aprendidas y Mejoras

  • Uso de IA: La combinación de actualizaciones automáticas y columnas de texto/estado con autocompletar IA resultó ser una solución efectiva para superar las limitaciones de las automatizaciones respecto a su implementación en las fórmulas.

posts.author: amybronstein (Amy Bronstein)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone! We know many of you have been eagerly awaiting an update here, and we really appreciate your patience on this. I’m excited to share where things stand with formula column support in automations and workflows.

What’s live now:

  • You can now use formula column values as dynamic text in automation actions like Notify, Send Email, and Create Update. This means calculated values can now be part of your automated communication and updates.

What’s still on the way:

  • Formula columns can’t yet be used in triggers or conditions.
  • Support for mapping actions (like “Create item”) and expanded functionality in workflows is coming soon (more details to come).
  • We’re also exploring support for formulas in triggers and Mirror columns, this is still in the early research stage.

Thank you again for sticking with us, we’re truly grateful for your feedback and continued support, and will continue to work on giving timely updates. Your feedback plays a huge role in helping us build the features you care about most.

posts.author: workflowboost (Bartosz @ Workflow Boost)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

I know many of you are hoping for a native solution, and there have already been some great third-party apps mentioned here. However, I believe it adds value to mention our Column to Column app, which offers a high level of flexibility and can solve most (if not all) of the use cases discussed in this thread.

The app fully supports complex formula scenarios, including formulas that reference mirror columns, formulas that depend on other formulas, and the full range of formula syntax supported by monday.

App introduces plenty of automation recipes; however, the one especially worth highlighting is the automation to copy the formula column whenever it’s recalculated:

image988×605 98.2 KB

On top of that, the app includes a set of workflow automation blocks, such as:

  • Triggering a workflow when a formula is recalculated
  • Extracting a formula column value as text, date, or number. The extracted value can be later used in a workflow.
  • Performing a formula inside the workflow (based on columns and other variables) and returning the result as text, date, or number. The formula result can be later used in a workflow.

These blocks can be combined freely. A common use case is triggering a workflow only when a formula evaluates to a specific value saved in a column:

image1772×1413 171 KB

One more important note, the app offers unlimited usage on every plan and is fully hosted on monday’s infrastructure, so security aligns with monday standards.

If you have any questions or want to explore whether this fits your use case, feel free to reach out at contact@workflow-boost.com
